place: Leesburg CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: The community was founded around 1800 by the Lee brothers, ship‑carpenters from Egg Harbor who opened the first shipyard on the Maurice River.
Shipbuilding stayed cornerstone of Leesburg's economy into the 20th century, exemplified by the Whibco Shipyard founded Delaware Bay Shipyard in 1928 and employing 500 workers at its peak.
Congress designated 10.3 miles of the Maurice River as a National Scenic and Recreational River in 1994, with Vice President Al Gore presiding over the ceremony at Leesburg property.
Swedes from Delaware River settlements ventured into the area to hunt, fish, and harvest resources along the Maurice River, as noted in a 1698 account by Gabriel Thomas.
The region experiences a humid subtropical climate, with warm summers and cold winters, marked by a July high of 86°F (30°C) and a January low of 23°F (-5°C).
